pages.glm:
Minor : Hardware design for AI

Roll no.: M25Ai2117 Name: Ashish Risey

Q.1. Ans. Parameters: 110 M
Sentiment analysis
Batch size: 32
Sequence length: 128 tokens
Target throughput: ≥ 1500 influence/sec
Max allowed latency ≤ 25ms
Power budget: 300W
Model size in PPI: 220 MB
Estimated compute requirement per influence: 22 GFlops.

Q.2. Ans. If Throughput target = 1500 inf/sec × 22 GFlops/inf
= 63000 GFlops/

Batch compute = 32×22 = 704 GFlops/batch.

Latency constraint ≤ 25ms
Effective TFLOPs ≥ 704 GFlops = 28,160 GFlops
0.025 s

So we need 33 TFLOPs for throughput.
and more than 28.2 TFLOPs to sustain

Q.3. Need to consider throughput + latency + power + data movement

1) GPU-A (35 TFLOPs) Needs: 80% of peak to hit 28.2
very high high requirement, comm miss latency.

2) GPU-B: very high high power req.

Max allowed latency ≤ 25ms
Power budget: 300W
Model size in PPI: 220 MB
Estimated compute requirement per influence: 22 GFlops.

Q.2. Ans. If Throughput target = 1500 inf/sec × 22 GFlops/inf
= 63000 GFlops/

Batch compute = 32×22 = 704 GFlops/batch.

Latency constraint ≤ 25ms
Effective TFLOPs ≥ 704 GFlops = 28,160 GFlops
0.025 s

So we need 33 TFLOPs for throughput.
and more than 28.2 TFLOPs to sustain

Q.3. Need to consider throughput + latency + power + data movement

2) GPU-B: very high high power req.

pages.glm:
Power efficiency: A => 35/120 = 0.29 TF/w
D => 0.89 TF/w
B => 125/250 = 0.80 TF/w

More GPU D has just balance of all parameters.

GPU-B is also strong just costly.

pages.glm:
Q.2. Maximum latency pin input: 3ms.
clock frequency: 800 MHz.
one mac cycle pin compute unit.
Mad available parallel mac units: 400.
memory is not bottleneck.

Amb cycles/ip = 300MHz x 3ms.
= 900,000 cycles.

Needed MACs = 2.7 x 10^6 with p parallel macs
cycle = 2.7 x 10^6
P
P ≥ 2.7 x 10^6 ≥ 3
9 x 10^5
P ≥ 3.

pages.glm:
# Q.3. if ANS . AHainable performance = min (Peak computer, BW x AF)
$$AI = \frac{Peak compute}{BW} = \frac{50GFLOPS}{12GB/s}$$

If $AI < 4.17$ then memory bound otherwise compute bound.

For RF inference computation mode is small but fetching is expensive hence this is memory-bound

by ANS . Performance improves because higher throughput/lower latency
SRAM is on-chip and fast so frequent operations will be handled with SRAM

ANS . Because early true latency is always negative
throughput/lower latency
SRAM is on-chip and fast so frequent operations will be handled with SRAM

pages.glm:
# 9.4.4 Number of clusters; $k=7$
Feature dimension; $D=23$
Max data points per batch; $N=1024$
Distance metric: squared distance
$q8.8$ is 16 bit, fixed point $(8-8 \text{ int - fractional point})$
Sign bit + 7 integer bits + 8 fractional bits
Max iterations allowed; 12
Clock freq: 250MHz
DSP block: 96
External DRAM (4GB/s, 1 port)

Global bus

BRAM on chip
512KB
Control FSM

Multiples per distance (accumulation): $N \cdot K \cdot (D-1) = 1024 \times 7 \times 23 = 164,864$

Distance multiplies/iteration: $N \cdot K \cdot (D-1) = 1024 \times 7 \times 22 = 157,696$

Distance adds/iteration: $N \cdot K \cdot (D-1) = 1024 \times 7 \times 22 = 157,696$
pages.final:
# 9.4.4 Number of clusters; $k=7$
Feature dimension; $D=23$
Max data points per batch; $N=1024$
Distance metric: squared distance
$q8.8$ is 16 bit, fixed point $(8-8 \text{ int - fractional point})$
Sign bit + 7 integer bits + 8 fractional bits
Max iterations allowed; 12
Clock freq: 250MHz
DSP block: 96
External DRAM (4GB/s, 1 port)

Global bus

BRAM on chip
512KB
Control FSM

Multiples per distance (accumulation): $N \cdot K \cdot (D-1) = 1024 \times 7 \times 23 = 164,864$

Distance multiplies/iteration: $N \cdot K \cdot (D-1) = 1024 \times 7 \times 22 = 157,696$

Distance adds/iteration: $N \cdot K \cdot (D-1) = 1024 \times 7 \times 22 = 157,696$

pages.glm:
Centroid update per iteration:
Accumulate assigned points. $N \times D = 1024 \times 23$
$= 23,552$ adds.

Compute new centroids: $1/\text{count} = K \times D \text{ multiplications}$
$= 7 \times 23$
$= 161(\text{multiples})$

Total iterations $= 164,864 + 161 = 165,025$.
Adds/iter $= 157,696 + 23552 = 181,248$.
Total ops/iter $= 346,273$.

Westcase 12 iterations
Multi: $165,025 \times 12 = 1,980,300$
Adds: $181,248 \times 12 = 2,174,976$.
Total ops/iter $= 346,273$.

Westcase 12 iterations
Multi: $165,025 \times 12 = 1,980,300$
Adds: $181,248 \times 12 = 2,174,976$.
Total ops/iter $= 346,273$.

Time taken $= \frac{4.155 \times 10^6}{250 \times 10^6} = 0.01662 = 16.6 \text{ ms}$.

Distance and centroid update update cannot turn parallel.
pages.final:

Accumulate assigned points. $N \times D = 1024 \times 23$
$= 23,552$ adds.

Compute new centroids: $1/\text{count} = K \times D \text{ multiplications}$
$= 7 \times 23$
$= 161(\text{multiples})$

Total iterations $= 164,864 + 161 = 165,025$.
Adds/iter $= 157,696 + 23552 = 181,248$.
Total ops/iter $= 346,273$.

Westcase 12 iterations
Multi: $165,025 \times 12 = 1,980,300$
Adds: $181,248 \times 12 = 2,174,976$.
Total ops/iter $= 346,273$.

Westcase 12 iterations
Multi: $165,025 \times 12 = 1,980,300$
Adds: $181,248 \times 12 = 2,174,976$.
Total ops/iter $= 346,273$.

Time taken $= \frac{4.155 \times 10^6}{250 \times 10^6} = 0.01662 = 16.6 \text{ ms}$.

Distance and centroid update update cannot turn parallel.

pages.glm:
Q.5.1> Ans. Yes improving batch size improves tensor core utilization because it increases parallel work. So, more independent operation.
- Makes GEMM/conv, kernels larger
- So, APD can keep more warps/sms.
- Hence busy SMS (sutter utilization)

Can batch size be limited by memory (latency so, too large batch cause problem)

11> Ans. Block tiling; each thread block computes a tile of output (e.g. 128×128)

Warp tiling; each warp computes a suitable.

Convolution + Activation reduces intermediate memory usage.

Improves performance/energy.
